name: Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer, LLC
description_short: Atlanta-based law firm specializing in business and personal bankruptcy, debt restructuring, and litigation with 10 attorneys and 1000+ clients served since 2018.
description_long:
Rountree Leitman Klein & Geer, LLC is a full-service law firm established in 2018 in Atlanta that focuses on bankruptcy law, debt restructuring, collections, and litigation matters. The firm is led by four named partners—Will Rountree, Hal Leitman, David Klein, and Will Geer—who collectively bring decades of combined legal experience. The firm operates with 10 attorneys on staff and reports having helped thousands of clients navigate serious financial and legal challenges.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services across multiple chapters, including Chapter 7 and Chapter 11 for personal bankruptcy cases, and Chapter 7, 11, and 12 for business bankruptcies. Beyond bankruptcy filing, they provide debt restructuring guidance, debtor/creditor representation covering wage garnishment, bank garnishment, foreclosures, evictions, and collections matters. Their litigation practice extends to civil, real estate, and commercial disputes, including bench trials and appeals. They position themselves as providing personalized, client-centered counsel tailored to individual circumstances.

What distinguishes this firm is their explicit multi-practice approach combining bankruptcy with robust litigation capabilities, rather than offering bankruptcy filing as an isolated service. They emphasize strategic counsel and personalized attention from named partners, and their 1000+ client count suggests established market presence in the Atlanta bankruptcy market. The firm maintains an active online presence with a professional website, intake forms, and multiple contact methods (phone and email), indicating accessibility for prospective clients.

As a licensed law firm, RLKG operates under ethical and regulatory requirements that provide client protections unavailable from non-attorney debt services. However, potential clients should note that law firm representation typically involves higher costs than non-legal debt relief alternatives. The website provides limited detail on fee structures, success rates, or client testimonials, which are important factors when selecting bankruptcy counsel. Consumers should verify the firm's standing with the Georgia State Bar and conduct independent due diligence before engagement.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Four named partners with decades of combined legal experience provide partner-level oversight
Full-service offerings spanning business bankruptcy, personal bankruptcy, debt restructuring, and commercial litigation reduce need for multiple providers
Covers multiple bankruptcy chapters (7, 11, 12) providing flexible solutions for different financial situations
Handles specialized debtor/creditor matters including wage garnishment, foreclosures, and evictions—common concerns for distressed individuals
Established firm (2018) with 1000+ clients served indicates market presence and repeat client relationships
Trial and appellate litigation experience suggests ability to handle contested bankruptcy cases beyond routine filings
Multiple contact methods (phone, email, intake form) and online scheduling show client accessibility
cons: Website provides no fee structure, pricing information, or payment plans—critical information for debt-distressed clients
No client testimonials, case outcomes, or success metrics published to evaluate actual performance
Limited detail on bankruptcy-specific expertise (e.g., which partner specializes in what chapter types)
Atlanta-based location may limit accessibility for out-of-state clients despite being licensed to practice
Law firm representation typically costs significantly more than non-attorney debt consolidation or settlement alternatives
best_for: Small business owners facing Chapter 11 reorganization or Chapter 7 liquidation with complex assets
High-income individuals with contested debt or litigation components (foreclosures, wage garnishments) requiring attorney advocacy
Debtors needing integrated bankruptcy plus commercial litigation services from a single firm
Atlanta-area residents with business bankruptcy needs combined with other civil legal matters
diagnosis: RLKG is best for Atlanta-area business owners and higher-income individuals requiring attorney-led bankruptcy representation with integrated litigation support. The main caveat is that as a law firm, their services carry higher costs than alternative debt relief options, and critical fee and outcome information is absent from their website—prospective clients must request detailed quotes and references before committing.
services: Chapter 7 business bankruptcy filing and administration
Civil, real estate, and commercial litigation with trial and appellate advocacy
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
